title: hesperis pendula subsp. campicarpa
intro: Hesperis pendula subsp. campicarpa, also known as rock rocket or climbing rocket, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its climbing habit and blue, purple, or white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect wounds. The health benefits of Hesperis pendula subsp. campicarpa include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve digestive issues, and act as a natural antiseptic to promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
